finding: Two distinct prices share the same label 'Indicative Price'. The live chart shows 6,581.85 SGD/oz, but the executed Buy/Sell uses 6,128.50 SGD/oz (30,642.50 ÷ 5). The user is never told which figure they will actually transact at.
novice_impact: Will believe the headline 6,581.85 is what they pay/receive. When the executed price (~7% lower) appears on the order history, they will feel misled and may dispute the trade.
seasoned_impact: Will recognise the gap as undisclosed spread/markup but cannot reconcile it without a breakdown. Erodes trust; experienced investors will trade elsewhere.
recommendation: Rename one of the two prices. Use 'Live indicative price' on the chart (with an explicit 'For reference only — your executed price may differ') and 'Executed price' on Review/Acknowledgement/Order History. Show both numbers side-by-side on Review with the SGD delta explained.

finding: No bid / ask spread is shown anywhere. Buy and Sell both transact off the same single 'Indicative Price' number, which means the spread (and any markup) is hidden inside the price. Fee, FX, and spread are all collapsed into one figure.
novice_impact: Cannot evaluate cost-of-trading — assumes the displayed price is the all-in cost.
seasoned_impact: Major credibility issue — every other gold/precious-metal product discloses a Buy and a Sell quote. Will assume the worst case (wide hidden spread) and trade elsewhere.
recommendation: Show a Buy quote and a Sell quote (bid/ask) on every chart and form header. On Review, break out: unit price × quantity = subtotal, ± fee, ± FX adjustment if applicable, = total.

finding: No fee / commission line item appears at any commit point. Fees are referenced only in a collapsed 'Fees and charges' FAQ on the landing — not equivalent to point-of-sale disclosure.
novice_impact: Will discover fees retrospectively in the bank statement; high-friction complaint risk.
seasoned_impact: Cannot model net P&L without knowing the fee per ounce or per trade.
recommendation: Add an explicit 'Fees & charges' row to the Review summary even if the fee is zero (state '0.00 SGD — included in price' if baked in). Disclose any GST/VAT on the same row.

finding: No risk warning is surfaced before commitment. Risk content is buried inside a collapsed 'Things to look out for' FAQ at the bottom of the landing. Charts show gain in green prominently; volatility / downside has no equivalent visual treatment.
novice_impact: May invest without understanding precious metals are not capital-guaranteed and price can fall sharply. Direct regulatory and complaint risk.
seasoned_impact: Notes the asymmetry between marketing prominence and risk prominence; questions the product's regulatory posture.
recommendation: Add a persistent risk panel above the chart on Buy/Sell forms: 'Precious metal prices can fall as well as rise. Past performance is not indicative of future results. Holdings are not SDIC-insured.' Plus a Key Fact Sheet link.

finding: The global header shows a 'Tasks and Statuses' pill (implying maker-checker workflow common in corporate banking), yet no 'submitted', 'pending approval', 'awaiting checker', or 'rejected' state is designed in any flow. The Buy acknowledgement jumps straight to 'filled successfully' from a single user click.
novice_impact: If the customer is the maker, they will not know the trade is awaiting a second-pair-of-eyes approval — may try to re-submit.
seasoned_impact: Same — and seasoned corporate operators will not trust a flow that hides workflow state.
recommendation: Design the four maker-checker states (Submitted / Pending Approval / Approved-Pending Settlement / Rejected) as Order History row statuses and as detail-screen statuses. The acknowledgement after Confirm should say 'Order submitted for approval' when maker-checker applies.

finding: No error / inline-validation, insufficient-balance, market-closed, price-stale, network-failure, or order-rejected states are designed in any of the 14 frames despite multiple form fields with min/max constraints and a real-money trade-execution context.
novice_impact: Will hit raw browser-default error messages or dead-ends with no recovery path. Likely to drop the trade or call support.
seasoned_impact: Will not trust a trading product whose failure modes are invisible.
recommendation: Design the full state matrix before launch: field-level validation (min/max, decimals, account-currency mismatch); page-level errors (insufficient SGD balance, insufficient XAU holding for sell, market closed, price stale > N seconds, network/API failure, order rejected by checker). Each state needs a recovery action.

finding: The single most consequential word in the product — 'Indicative' — is never explained inline. It appears in section headers, table columns, and price tiles, but no tooltip, helper, or glossary tells the user that 'indicative' means non-binding and the executed price may differ.
novice_impact: Reads 'Indicative price' as 'current price' — does not realise the executed price will differ.
seasoned_impact: Understands the term, but expects the bank to define it for compliance reasons.
recommendation: Add an inline info-icon next to every 'Indicative' label opening a tooltip: 'For reference only. The price you transact at is set at the moment of execution and may differ.'

finding: T&C consent is passive copy ('By clicking Submit you confirm you have read…'), not an explicit checkbox. There is no scroll-to-bottom requirement and no visible link-out to the full T&Cs in-flow.
novice_impact: Implicit consent — may not realise they are agreeing to anything.
seasoned_impact: Recognises this is below industry standard for regulated investment products.
recommendation: Replace passive copy with an explicit unchecked checkbox: '☐ I have read and agree to the Terms and Conditions and the Key Fact Sheet'. Disable the Confirm CTA until both are checked.

finding: The disclaimer copy says 'By clicking Submit, you confirm…' but the actual button is labelled 'Confirm Buy Order' (and 'Confirm Sell Order' on the Sell flow). The reference to 'Submit' breaks the user's mental link between consent and action.
novice_impact: Mild confusion; may wonder if there is a separate Submit step.
seasoned_impact: Reads as careless copy work, undermines trust.
recommendation: Change the disclaimer to reference the actual button label, OR rename the button to 'Submit'. Keep both consistent.

finding: No Key Fact Sheet (KFS) link, no suitability statement, and no product-knowledge gate exist anywhere in the flow. Standard regulated-investment requirement is missing.
novice_impact: Direct compliance risk — the user has not been shown the KFS before commitment.
seasoned_impact: Will expect KFS access on every regulated investment screen.
recommendation: Add a KFS link to the Review screen and to the Pre-Investment Landing card. For first-time precious-metal investors, gate Confirm behind a 5-question suitability check.

finding: Custody model (allocated vs unallocated) is never disclosed. The tagline 'Invest in gold and silver without holding the physical metal' implies unallocated paper bullion, but the bank's balance-sheet exposure model is never stated. Users cannot tell whether their holding is segregated, what happens on bank insolvency, or whether SDIC applies (it does not).
novice_impact: May assume their holding is physical or insured. Major mis-selling risk.
seasoned_impact: Will demand to know allocated vs unallocated before opening a position.
recommendation: Add a 'Custody and Protection' section to the Pre-Investment Landing and the Holding Details panel: 'Your holding is unallocated paper bullion — a contractual claim on OCBC, not segregated physical metal. Holdings are not SDIC-protected.'

finding: GST / tax treatment is never mentioned at point of sale. Singapore investment-precious-metal (IPM) gold ≥99.5% purity is GST-exempt, but silver and non-IPM gold attract GST. Silence at commit creates risk of unexpected tax line on statement.
novice_impact: Will not know whether the displayed price is GST-inclusive.
seasoned_impact: Will assume the worst and trade elsewhere if not stated.
recommendation: Add a 'GST treatment' line to the Review summary: 'GST: 0.00 SGD (IPM gold is GST-exempt)' or '+9% GST (XX SGD)' as appropriate. Same line should appear in the email confirmation.

finding: Order History rows are not clickable — there is no row-level chevron, no 'View' action, and no order-detail screen exists in the deck. Users cannot drill into an order to see breakdown, fees, settlement, or the termsheet.
novice_impact: Cannot retrieve their own trade record beyond the columns shown.
seasoned_impact: Cannot pull termsheet or execution detail for reconciliation, accounting, or audit.
recommendation: Make every row clickable to an Order Detail screen showing: full economics (price, qty, fee, FX, GST, total), settlement status, termsheet PDF link, and (for corporate) maker/checker history.

finding: No 'Status' column on the Order History table. There is no way to distinguish filled / pending / cancelled / awaiting-approval / rejected orders. All visible rows look the same.
novice_impact: Cannot tell whether a recent order is settled or in flight.
seasoned_impact: Major operational gap — corporate ops needs status to reconcile.
recommendation: Add a STATUS column with coloured pills (Filled / Pending Approval / Awaiting Settlement / Cancelled / Rejected) and make it filterable.

finding: The risk / T&C disclaimer on the Review screen is rendered in plain ~12 px grey body text with no icon, no border, no background tint. It is visually weaker than the Buy chip, the summary band, and the dark Confirm button. For a regulated-investment commit screen, this is a serious trust signal gap.
novice_impact: Eye skips the disclaimer entirely — implicit consent without visual prominence.
seasoned_impact: Reads it but notes the weak treatment as below regulatory norms.
recommendation: Wrap the disclaimer in a tinted panel with a left border (e.g. amber #FEF3C7 / #F59E0B) and an icon, immediately above the Confirm CTA. Add the explicit T&C checkbox from Finding #8.

finding: Numerical columns (QUANTITY, INDICATIVE PRICE, TOTAL) are left-aligned and use proportional (not tabular) lining figures. Decimals do not line up vertically; finance-table convention requires right-alignment with tabular figures so digit-place comparison is possible.
novice_impact: Harder to scan but still readable.
seasoned_impact: Recognised as substandard finance-table styling — fast scanning of price/quantity columns is impaired.
recommendation: Right-align all numeric columns. Use tabular lining figures (CSS: font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums). Apply same rule to the Holdings Details panel and price tiles.

finding: Primary CTAs ('Review Buy Order', 'Confirm Buy Order', 'Create Alert') are dark slate (~#3F4A55) — the visually quietest element on the screen. The most important action does not compete with the chart card on the left or the Buy chip at top of the right column.
novice_impact: May not realise where to submit; eye lingers on the chart instead of the form.
seasoned_impact: Less harmful but still slows scanning of the action surface.
recommendation: Promote primary CTA to a brand-coloured filled button (OCBC red or a brand-blue) — at minimum, raise contrast and scale slightly. Use slate for secondary 'Back'.

finding: There is no price-validity window communicated. Users cannot tell whether the indicative price shown at form-fill is the same as at confirmation, and slippage between Review and Confirm is invisible.
novice_impact: Will be surprised when the executed price differs from the form price.
seasoned_impact: Expects a 'quote held for X seconds' countdown on every quote-driven trade.
recommendation: Either (a) lock the quote on Review with a visible countdown (e.g. 'Price held for 30s'), or (b) re-quote on Confirm with explicit 'Price has updated — confirm new price?' interrupt.

finding: No 'Past performance is not indicative of future results' disclaimer is shown near the price chart on Buy/Sell forms or on the Price Alert form. Standard regulated-finance boilerplate is missing wherever a chart is rendered.
novice_impact: May extrapolate past trend into expected future performance.
seasoned_impact: Recognised as a compliance gap.
recommendation: Add the boilerplate immediately under every chart: 'Past performance is not indicative of future results. Precious metal prices can fall as well as rise.'

finding: On the Buy/Sell form, the eye lands on the Gold (XAU) price card on the left first because it is the largest white-card-with-bold-numbers on the screen. The form on the right (the user's actual task) is visually quieter than the chart.
novice_impact: Wastes attention on the chart before realising the form is the task.
seasoned_impact: Mild inefficiency.
recommendation: Reduce the chart card visual weight (smaller header, less saturated chart fill) and elevate the form (e.g. subtle elevation/shadow or accent border at the top). Or invert the columns so the form is left-anchored.

finding: Buy form labels the SGD field 'Investment Amount'; Sell form labels the same SGD field 'Market Value'. The Sell email then reverts to 'Investment Amount' for what was 'Market Value' in-app. Same value, three different labels across the journey.
novice_impact: Adds unnecessary cognitive load — must re-learn labels per step.
seasoned_impact: Reads as careless copy work; questions whether the values truly mean the same thing.
recommendation: Pick one label and use it everywhere. Recommend 'Proceeds (SGD)' for Sell flows and 'Cost (SGD)' for Buy flows — both more accurate than the current labels and unambiguous between the two paths.

finding: The swap icon between 'Investment Amount' and 'Quantity to Buy' fields is unlabelled. Bi-directional auto-calculation (typing one fills the other) is implicit; novices won't know to use it.
novice_impact: May type both fields manually and produce inconsistent values, then hit a validation error.
seasoned_impact: Will infer the behaviour quickly.
recommendation: Add a tooltip on the swap icon: 'Quantity and amount are linked — fill either, the other will calculate automatically.' Or remove the swap and auto-fill the inactive field on blur.

finding: The 'Show available balance' link appears under the Credit Account on the filled Buy form, but is missing from the empty Buy form (where users would benefit before typing) and entirely absent from the Sell form. Either the Sell flow needs an equivalent 'Show available holdings' link, or the Buy/Sell paths have visibly drifted.
novice_impact: Cannot quickly check balance/holding before deciding amount.
seasoned_impact: Same — and notes the inconsistency between paths.
recommendation: Show 'Available balance: X SGD' inline under Credit Account on the Buy form (empty + filled). Show 'Available holding: X ounces' inline under the metal account on the Sell form. Same component, mirrored.

finding: Min/max guardrails are asymmetric. Buy form shows a Minimum (24.59 SGD, 0.01 ounces) but no Maximum (no exposure cap, no available-balance hard-stop label). Sell form shows a Maximum (25.86 ounces, 158,484.99 SGD) but no Minimum. Users cannot tell at a glance what the full bounds of each field are.
novice_impact: Will trial-and-error their way into validation errors.
seasoned_impact: Mild annoyance — expects both bounds visible.
recommendation: Show both Min and Max for every numeric field on both forms. Format: 'Min 0.01 oz · Max 25.86 oz'.
